evals.prompt: I want to add a 'duplicate project' feature. Users should be able to clone an existing project with all its tasks and members. Where should it live?
evals.expected_output: Routes through Step 1 (context), Step 2 (language alignment: project, task, member, duplicate), Step 3 (decisions: deep vs shallow clone, naming collision, permissions), Step 4 ('Blueprint ready'), Step 4.5 (write the design doc to docs/designs/<slug>-design.md with Status, Language, Decisions, Assumptions, Out of scope, What we are building), Step 4.6 (single pressure-test pass — subagent reads the design file directly, returns Risk Register), Step 4.7 (inline-edits the design file based on findings, appends Risk Register, updates Status), Step 4.8 (spec self-review), Step 4.9 (Design Review Gate — chat reply names the file path and current Status, hands control back, STOPS). Does NOT draft the plan, write to docs/plans/, or present the execution mode menu in the same turn. Does NOT re-spawn the subagent.
expectations: Asks language-alignment questions before proposing implementation
Surfaces at least 3 meaningful decisions (data ownership, naming, permissions, etc.)
Writes the design doc to docs/designs/ as the first action of the design phase (Step 4.5), BEFORE the pressure-test runs
Design file includes a ## Status block, Language, Decisions, Assumptions, Out of scope, and a one-paragraph 'What we are building' summary
Chat reply after writing the design file names the path and gives a one-line summary — does NOT paste the design contents
Spawns the challenge subagent AFTER the design doc is written; the subagent reads the file directly (Step 4.6, single pass only)
Inline-edits the design file based on the Risk Register (Step 4.7) — not a chat-only revision
After pressure-test, appends the Risk Register to the design file and updates the ## Status block to 'DRAFT — awaiting design approval' or 'OPEN SHOWSTOPPERS — awaiting design approval'
Does NOT re-spawn the subagent in a loop — single pass only
At Step 4.9 hands control back with a phrase like 'design ready for review at <path>'
Does NOT draft the plan, write to docs/plans/, or present the execution mode menu in the same turn

evals.prompt: Tiny typo fix: the 'Logiin' heading should be 'Login'.
evals.expected_output: Skips architect entirely. Notes it's a trivial fix and proceeds without a multi-step plan, language alignment, design doc, pressure-test, or execution mode menu.
expectations: Does NOT run Steps 1-6 of architect
Recognizes this as a one-line fix and skips the skill
Does NOT ask language-alignment questions or propose multi-file changes (negated mentions are OK)
May mention to just make the change
Does NOT write a design doc or spawn a challenge subagent (it would be wasted work for a typo)

evals.prompt: We need to add role-based access control across the app. 6 user roles, ~40 protected routes, complex permission inheritance. Will take weeks.
evals.expected_output: Routes through Steps 1-4 of the architect skill. Writes the design doc to docs/designs/<slug>-design.md as the first action of the design phase. Runs a single pressure-test pass on the file, inline-edits based on findings, and reaches Step 4.9 (Design Review Gate) with the file on disk. The chat reply names the design file path and current ## Status; it does NOT paste the full design. STOPS and waits for design approval. Does NOT write the plan file in the same turn.
expectations: Surfaces decisions about RBAC model (role hierarchy, permission inheritance, route guard placement, etc.)
Writes the design file to docs/designs/ as the first action (Step 4.5), BEFORE the pressure-test runs
Runs a single pressure-test pass on the file (Step 4.6); subagent reads the file directly
Inline-edits the design file based on the Risk Register (Step 4.7)
Reaches Step 4.9 with the design file on disk, Risk Register appended, ## Status set, and stops before writing any plan
Chat reply points to the design file path with current Status; does NOT paste the full design
Design file includes Status, Language, Decisions, Assumptions, Out of scope, Risk Register, and a What-we-are-building summary
Plan file is NOT written in the same turn as the design file
Uses delta notation or acknowledges that future updates will use ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED markers
Does NOT re-spawn the subagent in a loop

evals.prompt: Add a 'login with Google' button to the homepage. Use OAuth. We already have user accounts.
evals.expected_output: The model aligns on language, surfaces a design, and writes the design doc to docs/designs/<slug>-design.md BEFORE the pressure-test runs. The pressure-test (single pass) should catch an obvious hole — likely that the design doesn't address: token storage/session strategy, what happens to existing user accounts when they first log in via Google (account linking), logout flow, or revocation. The model then inline-edits the design file to address the finding (resolvable case) OR the final Risk Register shows it as an Open Showstopper / Concern the developer can see in the file.
expectations: Writes the design doc to docs/designs/ BEFORE the pressure-test runs
Pressure-test Risk Register (visible in the design file) flags at least one of: account linking strategy, session/token storage, logout, or revocation handling
If the showstopper is resolvable, the model inline-edits the design file to address it
If not resolvable, the model adds an Open Showstoppers section to the design file with Options (a)/(b)/(c) for the developer
Concerns stay in the Risk Register inside the design file for the developer's awareness
Does NOT just rubber-stamp the OAuth design without flagging the account-linking or session concerns
Does NOT paste the design contents in the chat — only the file path

evals.prompt: Add a 'user profile page' at /users/:id that shows the user's name, avatar, bio, and recent activity. We use Postgres for storage and React for the frontend.
evals.expected_output: The model aligns on language, surfaces decisions, and writes the design doc to docs/designs/<slug>-design.md BEFORE the pressure-test runs. The pressure-test (single pass) should produce a Showstopper or Concern about 'recent activity' — what counts as activity, where it comes from, how far back, performance implications, N+1 queries on profile loads. Because the answer IS in scope (model can decide on a reasonable interpretation), the model inline-edits the design file to scope 'recent activity' explicitly OR the final Risk Register in the file has it as a Concern with a one-line scoping decision. Tests the 'inline-edit resolves the showstopper' path.
expectations: Writes the design doc to docs/designs/ BEFORE the pressure-test runs
The Risk Register in the design file flags at least one concern about 'recent activity' (scope, source, performance, N+1)
The model inline-edits the design file to address the concern OR lists it as a Concern in the final Risk Register with a one-line scoping decision
Does NOT just accept 'recent activity' as a black box without challenge
Does NOT re-spawn the subagent — single pass only
Does NOT paste the design contents in the chat — only the file path

evals.prompt: After the design for 'duplicate project' has been approved, draft the implementation plan.
evals.expected_output: Routes through Step 5 (write the plan doc to docs/plans/<slug>-plan.md, mirroring language/decisions/assumptions from the design doc, with ordered Implementation steps, Files touched, and a ## Status block set to 'DRAFT — awaiting pressure-test'). Runs a single pressure-test pass on the plan file (Step 5.1) — subagent reads the file directly and looks for gaps in implementation steps. Inline-edits the plan file based on findings (Step 5.2), appends Risk Register, updates ## Status to 'DRAFT — awaiting plan approval' or 'OPEN SHOWSTOPPERS — awaiting plan approval'. Reaches Step 5.3 (Plan Review Gate) and STOPS. The chat reply names the plan file path and current Status; it does NOT paste the full plan.
expectations: Writes the plan doc to docs/plans/ as the first action of the plan phase (Step 5), BEFORE the pressure-test runs
Plan file mirrors Language, Decisions, Assumptions, and Out-of-scope from the design doc
Plan file includes ordered Implementation steps, a flat Files touched list, and a ## Status block
Updates the design file's ## Status block to 'DESIGN APPROVED — plan at docs/plans/<file>.md'
Runs a single pressure-test pass on the plan file (Step 5.1); subagent reads the file directly
Inline-edits the plan file based on the Risk Register (Step 5.2) — not a chat-only revision
After pressure-test, appends the Risk Register to the plan file and updates the ## Status block
Does NOT re-spawn the subagent — single pass only
At Step 5.3 hands control back with a phrase like 'plan ready for review at <path>'
Does NOT start implementation or present the execution mode menu in the same turn
